name: Samjokgu
description: The Samjokgu, or 'three-legged dog,' is a mythical creature prominent in Korean mythology and folklore. It is depicted as a canine with three legs, often possessing supernatural abilities and symbolic significance. While not as widely known as some other Korean mythical beings, the Samjokgu appears in various historical records and artistic representations, particularly during the Goguryeo period. Its imagery is often associated with the sun, representing the sun's power and journey across the sky. The three legs are believed to symbolize the three realms of existence: heaven, earth, and humanity, or alternatively, representing the past, present, and future. The Samjokgu is seen as a guardian spirit, warding off evil and bringing good fortune. Its unusual appearance and symbolic meaning make it a fascinating and unique element of Korean mythology, showcasing the rich cultural heritage and imaginative storytelling traditions of the Korean peninsula. Different interpretations of the Samjokgu exist across various regions and historical periods, but the core concept of a three-legged canine imbued with solar power and protective qualities remains consistent.
subtitle: Three-Legged Solar Guardian Dog
appearance: The Samjokgu is depicted as a dog-like creature, typically with fur ranging in color from fiery red to golden yellow, symbolizing its connection to the sun. Its most distinctive feature is its three legs, which are strong and allow for swift movement. The eyes often glow with an inner light, reflecting its solar power. It may also have a mane of flames or a halo of light surrounding its head. Overall, its appearance is majestic and imposing, inspiring both awe and respect.
habitat: The Samjokgu is often associated with celestial realms, particularly the sun. It may also inhabit mountainous regions, forests, or sacred places where it acts as a guardian. It is not tied to a specific location but rather appears where its protective presence is needed.
behavior: The Samjokgu is generally portrayed as a benevolent creature, fiercely loyal and protective of those it guards. It is intelligent and perceptive, able to sense danger and foresee future events. While generally peaceful, it will fiercely defend its territory and those under its protection. It is often seen as a symbol of courage, loyalty, and unwavering dedication.

keyMyths.title: The Sun's Guardian
keyMyths.description: The Samjokgu is said to pull the sun across the sky each day, ensuring its light reaches all corners of the world and protecting it from darkness. Its three legs represent its ability to traverse the celestial realm with ease.

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy of the Three-Legged Dog
keyMyths.description: Legend tells of a Samjokgu appearing before a great disaster, warning the people of impending doom and guiding them to safety. Its presence is a sign of both impending danger and hope for survival.

sources.author: Il-yeon
sources.work: Samguk Yusa (Memorabilia of the Three Kingdoms)
sources.description: A collection of legends, folktales, and historical accounts relating to the Three Kingdoms of Korea.
